Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company'S Motion To Preclude Any Evidence Of Exposure Allegations Against It Made By Plaintiff After The Close Of Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kathryn J. LaFevers, Esq. of GORDON & REES (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / moving party The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Joseph Tocchini

RULING:
Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion to preclude any evidence of exposure allegations against it made after the close of discovery. Counsel submitted to the tentative ruling and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as amended: Defendant Goodyear's motion is granted in part. Plaintiff wishes to support his claims against Goodyear by introducing evidence of his exposure to Cranite, the trial is continued to enable Goodyear to take appropriate discovery and file a summary judgment motion as to Cranite exposure. Defendant can file a summary judgment motion with a 30-day notice. Plaintiff's failure to serve his June 2008 responses to Crane Co.'s discovery on Goodyear coupled with what appears to be plaintiff's failure to have made a reasonable and good faith effort to include information regarding Cranite exposure in his June 2008 responses to Goodyear's discovery warrants exclusion of Cranite exposure evidence against Goodyear absent a trial continuance to enable Goodyear to take necessary discovery and have a motion for summary judgment heard. The trial is ordered continued to January 18, 2011 at 11:15 a.m. in Dept. 220. Counsel for Goodyear is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not Reported. = (220/HEK)
